title: str subclass instantiation under OOM frees a unicode with NULL data — unicode_subtype_new (Objects/unicodeobject.c:13986)
description: unicode_subtype_new allocates the str-subclass instance `self` then its data buffer; if a later allocation fails under OOM it jumps to onError: Py_DECREF(self) while _PyUnicode_DATA_ANY(self) is still NULL, so unicode_dealloc -> unicode_is_singleton -> _PyUnicode_NONCOMPACT_DATA asserts data != NULL and aborts.
notes: Assertion `data != NULL' in _PyUnicode_NONCOMPACT_DATA (unicodeobject.h:272), reached via unicode_dealloc->unicode_is_singleton when a str-subclass `self` is freed on unicode_subtype_new's onError path with self->data still NULL. Same partially-constructed-object-freed-on-OOM class as OOM-0024 (template_iter); distinct from OOM-0009 (replace). Debug-only assert (Py_DEBUG); release dereferences the NULL data (segfault/UAF risk). Found by 2 fleet instances via email._header_value_parser str-subclass construction. Fix: leave the subtype in a dealloc-safe (empty/valid data) state right after tp_alloc, or make unicode_is_singleton/unicode_dealloc tolerate NULL data. MINIMAL REPRO (shrinkray-reduced from the vehicle, deterministic, re-verified 40x): `email._header_value_parser.get_value("\x00")` under the set_nomemory sweep. (An earlier hand reduction `class S(str): S('...')` did not hit the window; shrinkray found the email get_value trigger.) First bugs found by the LOCAL systemd fleet against the then-29-bug catalog.
